{"description": "Build itinerary backend module", "prompt": "Build the full itinerary domain module for the Go Echo backend at /c/Users/yonim/w2t80/repo/apps/api/internal/modules/itineraries/. 

Context: This is a Travel Platform modular monolith. The backend uses Echo, pgx/v5 for PostgreSQL, zap for logging. The module path is `travel-platform/apps/api/internal/modules/itineraries`.

Common package is at `travel-platform/apps/api/internal/common` with:
- `common.Success(c, data)`, `common.Created(c, data)`, `common.Error(c, status, code, message)`, `common.ValidationError(c, errors)`
- `common.GetUserID(c)`, `common.GetRoles(c)`, `common.GetRequestID(c)`
- `common.DomainError{Code, Message, Err}`, `common.NewNotFoundError(resource)`, etc.

Middleware package at `travel-platform/apps/api/internal/middleware` has:
- `middleware.RequireRole(roles ...string) echo.MiddlewareFunc`

Database tables (already exist in migrations):
- itineraries (id UUID, organizer_id UUID FK users, title VARCHAR, meetup_at TIMESTAMPTZ, meetup_location_text VARCHAR, notes TEXT, status itinerary_status enum, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at, updated_at)
- itinerary_checkpoints (id UUID, itinerary_id UUID FK, sort_order INT, checkpoint_text VARCHAR, eta TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, created_at, updated_at)
- itinerary_members (id UUID, itinerary_id UUID FK, user_id UUID FK, role VARCHAR, joined_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE(itinerary_id, user_id))
- itinerary_member_form_definitions (id UUID, itinerary_id UUID FK, field_key VARCHAR, field_label VARCHAR, field_type VARCHAR, required BOOLEAN, options_json JSONB, validation_json JSONB, active BOOLEAN, sort_order INT, created_at, updated_at)
- itinerary_member_form_submissions (id UUID, itinerary_id UUID FK, member_user_id UUID FK, payload_json JSONB, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at)
- itinerary_change_events (id UUID, itinerary_id UUID FK, actor_id UUID FK, change_type VARCHAR, summary TEXT, diff_json JSONB, visible_from TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at) -- append only
- itinerary_notifications (id UUID, itinerary_id UUID FK, recipient_user_id UUID FK, message TEXT, read_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at)

Itinerary status enum: draft/published/revised/in_progress/completed/cancelled/archived

Create these files:

## 1. model.go
Define Go structs for all entities. Use `*time.Time` for nullable timestamps, `json.RawMessage` for JSONB fields. Include JSON tags.

```go
type Itinerary struct { ... }
type Checkpoint struct { ... }
type Member struct { ... }
type FormDefinition struct { ... }
type FormSubmission struct { ... }
type ChangeEvent struct { ... }
```

## 2. dto.go  
Request/response DTOs:
- CreateItineraryRequest (title, meetupAt, meetupLocationText, notes)
- UpdateItineraryRequest (all optional)
- CreateCheckpointRequest (checkpointText, sortOrder, eta optional)
- UpdateCheckpointRequest
- AddMemberRequest (userId, role)
- CreateFormDefinitionRequest (fieldKey, fieldLabel, fieldType, required, options, validation, sortOrder)
- SubmitFormRequest (payload map)
- ItineraryResponse (full itinerary with checkpoints count, member count)
- ItineraryDetailResponse (with embedded checkpoints, members, form definitions)
- ChangeEventResponse

## 3. repository.go
CRUD operations using pgx pool directly:
- CreateItinerary(ctx, itinerary) (id, error)
- GetByID(ctx, id) (*Itinerary, error)
- List(ctx, filters) ([]Itinerary, total int, error) -- supports pagination, status filter, organizer filter
- Update(ctx, id, fields) error
- UpdateStatus(ctx, id, newStatus) error
- CreateCheckpoint(ctx, checkpoint) error
- GetCheckpoints(ctx, itineraryID) ([]Checkpoint, error)
- UpdateCheckpoint(ctx, id, fields) error
- DeleteCheckpoint(ctx, id) error
- AddMember(ctx, itineraryID, userID, role) error
- RemoveMember(ctx, itineraryID, userID) error
- GetMembers(ctx, itineraryID) ([]Member, error)
- IsMember(ctx, itineraryID, userID) (bool, error)
- CreateFormDefinition(ctx, def) error
- GetFormDefinitions(ctx, itineraryID) ([]FormDefinition, error)
- UpdateFormDefinition(ctx, id, fields) error
- SubmitForm(ctx, submission) error
- GetFormSubmissions(ctx, itineraryID) ([]FormSubmission, error)
- GetFormSubmissionByUser(ctx, itineraryID, userID) (*FormSubmission, error)
- CreateChangeEvent(ctx, event) error  -- append only
- GetChangeEvents(ctx, itineraryID, afterTime) ([]ChangeEvent, error)
- GetChangeEventsForUser(ctx, itineraryID, userID) ([]ChangeEvent, error) -- visible_from <= now

Use parameterized queries. Handle pgx.ErrNoRows appropriately.

## 4. service.go
Business logic layer:
- CreateItinerary(ctx, userID, req) (*ItineraryResponse, error)
- GetItinerary(ctx, id, userID) (*ItineraryDetailResponse, error) -- checks visibility
- ListItineraries(ctx, userID, roles, page, pageSize, status) (paginated response, error)
- UpdateItinerary(ctx, id, userID, req) error -- checks ownership, creates change event for material changes
- PublishItinerary(ctx, id, userID) error -- validates required fields, transitions status, notifies members
- AddCheckpoint(ctx, itineraryID, userID, req) error -- ownership check, creates change event
- UpdateCheckpoint(ctx, itineraryID, checkpointID, userID, req) error
- DeleteCheckpoint(ctx, itineraryID, checkpointID, userID) error
- AddMember(ctx, itineraryID, userID, req) error
- RemoveMember(ctx, itineraryID, userID, targetUserID) error
- CreateFormDefinition(ctx, itineraryID, userID, req) error
- UpdateFormDefinition(ctx, itineraryID, defID, userID, req) error
- SubmitForm(ctx, itineraryID, userID, req) error -- validates required fields from definitions
- GetChangeEvents(ctx, itineraryID, userID) ([]ChangeEventResponse, error)

Rules:
- Only organizer (or admin) can create/update/publish itineraries
- Members (participants) can view itineraries they belong to
- Every material change (meetup_at, meetup_location_text, notes, checkpoints add/remove/edit) must create a change event
- Change event summary should be human-readable like "Meetup time changed from X to Y"
- Publishing requires: title, meetup_at, meetup_location_text
- After publish, updates create change events and set status to 'revised'

## 5. handler.go
HTTP handlers that bind DTOs, call service, return responses:
- POST /api/v1/itineraries -> Create
- GET /api/v1/itineraries -> List (query params: page, pageSize, status)
- GET /api/v1/itineraries/:id -> GetByID
- PATCH /api/v1/itineraries/:id -> Update
- POST /api/v1/itineraries/:id/publish -> Publish
- POST /api/v1/itineraries/:id/checkpoints -> AddCheckpoint
- PATCH /api/v1/itineraries/:id/checkpoints/:checkpointId -> UpdateCheckpoint
- DELETE /api/v1/itineraries/:id/checkpoints/:checkpointId -> DeleteCheckpoint
- POST /api/v1/itineraries/:id/members -> AddMember
- DELETE /api/v1/itineraries/:id/members/:userId -> RemoveMember
- POST /api/v1/itineraries/:id/form-definitions -> CreateFormDefinition
- PATCH /api/v1/itineraries/:id/form-definitions/:defId -> UpdateFormDefinition
- GET /api/v1/itineraries/:id/form-definitions -> GetFormDefinitions
- POST /api/v1/itineraries/:id/form-submissions -> SubmitForm
- GET /api/v1/itineraries/:id/form-submissions -> GetFormSubmissions
- GET /api/v1/itineraries/:id/change-events -> GetChangeEvents

Include a RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group) method. Apply RBAC middleware:
- Create/update/publish: RequireRole("group_organizer", "administrator")
- View: any authenticated user (filtered by membership in service layer)
- Form submission: RequireRole("traveler", "group_organizer", "administrator")

## 6. policy.go
Authorization helpers:
- CanManageItinerary(userID string, roles []string, itinerary *Itinerary) bool
  - true if userID == itinerary.OrganizerID or roles contains "administrator"
- CanViewItinerary(userID string, roles []string, isMember bool, itinerary *Itinerary) bool
  - true if organizer, admin, or isMember

After creating all files, verify compilation: `cd /c/Users/yonim/w2t80/repo/apps/api && export PATH="/c/Go/bin:$PATH" && go build ./...`

IMPORTANT: Write production-quality Go code. Use proper error handling. Use `github.com/google/uuid` for UUID generation. Use `github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool` for DB access.", "run_in_background": true}

- GetFormSubmissions(ctx, itineraryID) ([]FormSubmission, error)
- GetFormSubmissionByUser(ctx, itineraryID, userID) (*FormSubmission, error)
- CreateChangeEvent(ctx, event) error  -- append only
- GetChangeEvents(ctx, itineraryID, afterTime) ([]ChangeEvent, error)
- GetChangeEventsForUser(ctx, itineraryID, userID) ([]ChangeEvent, error) -- visible_from <= now

Use parameterized queries. Handle pgx.ErrNoRows appropriately.

## 4. service.go
Business logic layer:
- CreateItinerary(ctx, userID, req) (*ItineraryResponse, error)
- GetItinerary(ctx, id, userID) (*ItineraryDetailResponse, error) -- checks visibility
- ListItineraries(ctx, userID, roles, page, pageSize, status) (paginated response, error)
- UpdateItinerary(ctx, id, userID, req) error -- checks ownership, creates change event for material changes
- PublishItinerary(ctx, id, userID) error -- validates required fields, transitions status, notifies members
- AddCheckpoint(ctx, itineraryID, userID, req) error -- ownership check, creates change event
- UpdateCheckpoint(ctx, itineraryID, checkpointID, userID, req) error
- DeleteCheckpoint(ctx, itineraryID, checkpointID, userID) error
- AddMember(ctx, itineraryID, userID, req) error
- RemoveMember(ctx, itineraryID, userID, targetUserID) error
- CreateFormDefinition(ctx, itineraryID, userID, req) error
- UpdateFormDefinition(ctx, itineraryID, defID, userID, req) error
- SubmitForm(ctx, itineraryID, userID, req) error -- validates required fields from definitions
- GetChangeEvents(ctx, itineraryID, userID) ([]ChangeEventResponse, error)

Rules:
- Only organizer (or admin) can create/update/publish itineraries
- Members (participants) can view itineraries they belong to
- Every material change (meetup_at, meetup_location_text, notes, checkpoints add/remove/edit) must create a change event
- Change event summary should be human-readable like "Meetup time changed from X to Y"
- Publishing requires: title, meetup_at, meetup_location_text
- After publish, updates create change events and set status to 'revised'

## 5. handler.go
HTTP handlers that bind DTOs, call service, return responses:
- POST /api/v1/itineraries -> Create
- GET /api/v1/itineraries -> List (query params: page, pageSize, status)
- GET /api/v1/itineraries/:id -> GetByID
- PATCH /api/v1/itineraries/:id -> Update
- POST /api/v1/itineraries/:id/publish -> Publish
- POST /api/v1/itineraries/:id/checkpoints -> AddCheckpoint
- PATCH /api/v1/itineraries/:id/checkpoints/:checkpointId -> UpdateCheckpoint
- DELETE /api/v1/itineraries/:id/checkpoints/:checkpointId -> DeleteCheckpoint
- POST /api/v1/itineraries/:id/members -> AddMember
- DELETE /api/v1/itineraries/:id/members/:userId -> RemoveMember
- POST /api/v1/itineraries/:id/form-definitions -> CreateFormDefinition
- PATCH /api/v1/itineraries/:id/form-definitions/:defId -> UpdateFormDefinition
- GET /api/v1/itineraries/:id/form-definitions -> GetFormDefinitions
- POST /api/v1/itineraries/:id/form-submissions -> SubmitForm
- GET /api/v1/itineraries/:id/form-submissions -> GetFormSubmissions
- GET /api/v1/itineraries/:id/change-events -> GetChangeEvents

Include a RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group) method. Apply RBAC middleware:
- Create/update/publish: RequireRole("group_organizer", "administrator")
- View: any authenticated user (filtered by membership in service layer)
- Form submission: RequireRole("traveler", "group_organizer", "administrator")

## 6. policy.go
Authorization helpers:
- CanManageItinerary(userID string, roles []string, itinerary *Itinerary) bool
  - true if userID == itinerary.OrganizerID or roles contains "administrator"
- CanViewItinerary(userID string, roles []string, isMember bool, itinerary *Itinerary) bool
  - true if organizer, admin, or isMember

After creating all files, verify compilation: `cd /c/Users/yonim/w2t80/repo/apps/api && export PATH="/c/Go/bin:$PATH" && go build ./...`

content.name: Agent
content.arguments:
{"description": "Build pricing/checkout module", "prompt": "Build the pricing engine and checkout module for the Go Echo backend at /c/Users/yonim/w2t80/repo/apps/api/internal/modules/pricing/ and /c/Users/yonim/w2t80/repo/apps/api/internal/modules/bookings/.

Context: Travel Platform modular monolith using Echo, pgx/v5, zap. Module import root: `travel-platform/apps/api/internal/modules/pricing` and `travel-platform/apps/api/internal/modules/bookings`.

Common package at `travel-platform/apps/api/internal/common` provides:
- `common.Success(c, data)`, `common.Created(c, data)`, `common.Error(c, status, code, msg)`, `common.ValidationError(c, errs)`
- `common.GetUserID(c)`, `common.GetRoles(c)`
- DomainError types

Middleware at `travel-platform/apps/api/internal/middleware`: `middleware.RequireRole(roles ...string)`

Database tables (from migrations):
- bookings (id, organizer_id FK users, itinerary_id FK nullable, title, description, status booking_status, total_amount NUMERIC(18,2), discount_amount NUMERIC(18,2), escrow_amount NUMERIC(18,2), pricing_snapshot_id UUID nullable, created_at, updated_at)
- booking_items (id, booking_id FK, item_type, item_name, description, unit_price NUMERIC(18,2), quantity INT, subtotal NUMERIC(18,2), category, created_at)
- checkout_pricing_snapshots (id, booking_id FK nullable, procurement_order_id FK nullable, snapshot_json JSONB, created_at)
- coupons (id, code UNIQUE, name, discount_type enum(threshold_fixed/percentage/new_user_gift), amount NUMERIC(18,2), min_spend NUMERIC(18,2), percent_off NUMERIC(5,2), valid_from, valid_to, eligibility_json JSONB, stack_group, exclusive BOOLEAN, usage_limit_total INT, usage_limit_per_user INT, active BOOLEAN, created_at, updated_at)
- coupon_redemptions (id, coupon_id FK, user_id FK, booking_id nullable, procurement_order_id nullable, redemption_scope_key UNIQUE(coupon_id, user_id, redemption_scope_key), discount_amount, created_at)
- idempotency_keys (id, actor_id FK, key, route, request_hash, response_code INT, response_body_json JSONB, locked_at, created_at, expires_at, UNIQUE(actor_id, route, key))
- escrow_accounts (id, order_type, order_id, amount_held, amount_released, amount_refunded, status escrow_status)
- payment_records (id, order_type, order_id, tender_type enum, amount, currency, reference_text, recorded_by FK, recorded_at)
- journal_entries (id, entry_type, reference_type, reference_id, description, effective_at, created_by, created_at) -- immutable
- journal_lines (id, journal_entry_id FK, account_code, direction(debit/credit), amount, counterparty_id nullable) -- immutable

## PRICING MODULE (/modules/pricing/)

### model.go
Types:
- CouponEligibilityResult { CouponID, Code, Name, Eligible bool, Reason string, ReasonCode string, DiscountAmount }
- PricingResult { Subtotal, TotalDiscount, EscrowHoldAmount, FinalPayable, EligibleCoupons []AppliedCoupon, IneligibleCoupons []IneligibleCoupon, AppliedDiscounts []AppliedDiscount, SnapshotID string }
- AppliedCoupon { CouponID, Code, Name, DiscountAmount }
- IneligibleCoupon { CouponID, Code, Name, ReasonCode, Message string }
- AppliedDiscount { Type, Description, Amount string }

### engine.go
The core deterministic pricing engine. This is critical business logic.

Function: `EvaluateCheckout(ctx, items []BookingItem, couponCodes []string, userID string, isNewUser bool, membershipTier string) (*PricingResult, error)`

Algorithm (strict precedence order):
1. Calculate base subtotal from line items
2. Apply member pricing tier adjustment if applicable (pricing tier, NOT a coupon)
3. Evaluate each coupon for eligibility
4. Apply at most 1 threshold_fixed discount (e.g., "$25 off $200")
5. Apply at most 1 percentage discount (e.g., "10% off lodging")
6. If new_user_gift is eligible AND applies: DENY all other stackables, return explanation "New user gift is exclusive and cannot be combined with other discounts"
7. Compute final total
8. Return full result with explanations

### rules.go
Eligibility check functions:
- checkDateRange(coupon, now) -> (eligible bool, reason string)
- checkMinSpend(coupon, subtotal) -> (eligible, reason)
- checkCategoryRestriction(coupon, items) -> (eligible, reason)
- checkNewUserOnly(coupon, isNewUser) -> (eligible, reason)
- checkMembershipRequired(coupon, membershipTier) -> (eligible, reason)
- checkUsageLimitTotal(ctx, repo, couponID) -> (eligible, reason)
- checkUsageLimitPerUser(ctx, repo, couponID, userID) -> (eligible, reason)
- checkAlreadyRedeemed(ctx, repo, couponID, userID, scopeKey) -> (eligible, reason)

Reason codes (matching frontend enums):
- ROOM_TYPE_RESTRICTED, DATE_OUT_OF_RANGE, MIN_SPEND_NOT_MET, MEMBERSHIP_REQUIRED, NEW_USER_ONLY, STACKING_NOT_ALLOWED, USAGE_LIMIT_REACHED, EXPIRED, INACTIVE, CATEGORY_RESTRICTED, ALREADY_REDEEMED

Each reason code must have a human-friendly message.

### explanations.go
Map reason codes to user-friendly messages:
```go
var ReasonMessages = map[string]string{
    "DATE_OUT_OF_RANGE":    "This coupon is not valid for the selected dates",
    "MIN_SPEND_NOT_MET":    "Minimum spend of $%.2f is required",
    ...
}
```

### repository.go (for pricing)
- GetActiveCouponsByCodes(ctx, codes []string) ([]Coupon, error)
- GetCouponRedemptionCount(ctx, couponID string) (int, error)
- GetUserCouponRedemptionCount(ctx, couponID, userID string) (int, error)
- HasRedemption(ctx, couponID, userID, scopeKey string) (bool, error)
- SavePricingSnapshot(ctx, bookingID *string, snapshotJSON []byte) (snapshotID string, error)

### snapshots.go
- CreateSnapshot(result *PricingResult, items []BookingItem) ([]byte, error) - serializes to JSON

### idempotency.go
- CheckIdempotencyKey(ctx, pool, actorID, route, key, requestHash string) (found bool, response *IdempotencyResponse, error)
- LockIdempotencyKey(ctx, pool, actorID, route, key, requestHash string) error
- CompleteIdempotencyKey(ctx, pool, actorID, route, key, responseCode int, responseBody []byte) error

### handler.go
- GET /api/v1/coupons/available -> list active coupons
- POST /api/v1/coupons/evaluate -> evaluate eligibility for given codes + items
- POST /api/v1/coupons/redeem-preview -> full price preview with coupon application

RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group)

## BOOKINGS MODULE (/modules/bookings/)

### model.go
- Booking struct
- BookingItem struct  
- Escrow struct

### dto.go
- CreateBookingRequest { title, description, itineraryID optional, items []CreateBookingItemRequest }
- CreateBookingItemRequest { itemType, itemName, description, unitPrice, quantity, category }
- CheckoutRequest { pricingSnapshotID, couponCodes []string, idempotencyKey string }
- TenderRecordRequest { tenderType, amount, referenceText }
- BookingResponse, BookingItemResponse, CheckoutResponse

### repository.go
- Create(ctx, booking) (id, error)
- GetByID(ctx, id) (*Booking, error)
- GetItems(ctx, bookingID) ([]BookingItem, error)
- UpdateStatus(ctx, id, status) error
- UpdatePricingSnapshot(ctx, id, snapshotID) error
- CreateEscrow(ctx, escrow) error
- GetEscrow(ctx, orderType, orderID) (*Escrow, error)
- RecordTender(ctx, record) error
- CreateCouponRedemption(ctx, redemption) error

### service.go
- CreateBooking(ctx, userID, req) (*BookingResponse, error)
- GetBooking(ctx, id, userID) (*BookingResponse, error)
- PricePreview(ctx, bookingID, userID, couponCodes) (*PricingResult, error)
- Checkout(ctx, bookingID, userID, req CheckoutRequest) (*CheckoutResponse, error)
  THIS IS THE CRITICAL METHOD. Must:
  1. Require Idempotency-Key header (passed in req)
  2. Check idempotency key - if same key+same request hash, return cached response
  3. If same key+different hash, return error
  4. Lock idempotency key
  5. Within single DB transaction:
     a. Validate booking status (must be draft or pending_pricing)
     b. Fetch booking items
     c. Run pricing engine
     d. Validate pricing snapshot matches
     e. Create coupon redemptions (unique constraint prevents double-redeem)
     f. Create escrow hold
     g. Create journal entries (debit Cash On Hand, credit Escrow Liability)
     h. Update booking status to paid_held_in_escrow
     i. Save pricing snapshot
  6. Complete idempotency key with response
  7. Return checkout response
- RecordTender(ctx, bookingID, userID, req) error
- CancelBooking(ctx, bookingID, userID) error
- CompleteBooking(ctx, bookingID, userID) error -- releases escrow

### handler.go
- POST /api/v1/bookings -> Create
- GET /api/v1/bookings/:id -> GetByID
- POST /api/v1/bookings/:id/price-preview -> PricePreview
- POST /api/v1/bookings/:id/checkout -> Checkout (requires Idempotency-Key header)
- POST /api/v1/bookings/:id/record-tender -> RecordTender
- POST /api/v1/bookings/:id/cancel -> Cancel
- POST /api/v1/bookings/:id/complete -> Complete

RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group)

## Ledger helper (create at /modules/finance/ledger.go)
Create a minimal ledger posting function:
```go
func PostJournalEntry(ctx context.Context, tx pgx.Tx, entryType, refType string, refID uuid.UUID, description string, createdBy string, lines []JournalLine) error
```
Where JournalLine has: AccountCode, Direction(debit/credit), Amount, CounterpartyID
- Validates sum(debits) == sum(credits) before posting
- Inserts journal_entries + journal_lines within the transaction

Chart of accounts constants:
- CashOnHand = "1000"
- ManualTenderClearing = "1100"
- EscrowLiability = "2000"
- SupplierPayable = "2100"
- CourierPayable = "2200"
- CustomerWalletLiability = "2300"
- RefundLiability = "2400"
- Revenue = "4000"
- FeeRevenue = "4100"
- AdjustmentExpense = "5000"
- SettlementAdjustmentReserve = "2500"

After creating all files, compile: `cd /c/Users/yonim/w2t80/repo/apps/api && export PATH="/c/Go/bin:$PATH" && go build ./...`

IMPORTANT: 
- Money values must use `decimal` type or `float64` with care. Since we're using NUMERIC(18,2) in Postgres, represent as `float64` in Go structs and be careful with comparisons. Better yet, use string representation for amounts in DTOs and `float64` internally with rounding.
- The checkout must be truly idempotent and transactional.
- All journal entries must balance (debits == credits).
- Coupon stacking: max 1 threshold + max 1 percentage. New user gift is exclusive (never stacks).
- Minimum refund unit: $1.00.", "run_in_background": true}

- Money values must use `decimal` type or `float64` with care. Since we're using NUMERIC(18,2) in Postgres, represent as `float64` in Go structs and be careful with comparisons. Better yet, use string representation for amounts in DTOs and `float64` internally with rounding.
- The checkout must be truly idempotent and transactional.
- All journal entries must balance (debits == credits).
- Coupon stacking: max 1 threshold + max 1 percentage. New user gift is exclusive (never stacks).
- Minimum refund unit: $1.00.", "run_in_background": true}

content.arguments:
{"description": "Build notification/messaging module", "prompt": "Build the notification and messaging module for the Go Echo backend at /c/Users/yonim/w2t80/repo/apps/api/internal/modules/notifications/.

Context: Travel Platform using Echo + pgx/v5 + zap. Module path: `travel-platform/apps/api/internal/modules/notifications`.

Common package at `travel-platform/apps/api/internal/common` has standard response helpers, context helpers, and error types.

Database tables:
- message_templates (id UUID, template_key VARCHAR UNIQUE, subject_template TEXT, body_template TEXT, channel_type VARCHAR, active BOOLEAN, created_at, updated_at)
- messages (id UUID, sender_id UUID nullable, recipient_id FK users, subject VARCHAR, body TEXT, template_id UUID nullable, metadata_json JSONB, read_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at)
- notification_events (id UUID, event_type VARCHAR, source_type VARCHAR, source_id UUID, payload_json JSONB, created_at)
- notification_recipients (id UUID, event_id FK, user_id FK, channel VARCHAR DEFAULT 'in_app', status VARCHAR DEFAULT 'pending', delivered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, read_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deferred_until TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at)
- callback_queue_entries (id UUID, event_id UUID nullable, recipient_id UUID nullable, payload_json JSONB, status VARCHAR DEFAULT 'pending', attempts INT, last_attempted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, exported_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at)
- send_logs (id UUID, recipient_user_id FK, message_id UUID nullable, event_type VARCHAR, channel_type VARCHAR, status VARCHAR, payload_summary_json JSONB, created_at) -- IMMUTABLE
- message_read_receipts (id UUID, message_id FK, user_id FK, read_at)
- do_not_disturb_settings (user_id PK, dnd_start TIME DEFAULT '21:00', dnd_end TIME DEFAULT '08:00', enabled BOOLEAN)
- subscription_preferences (id UUID, user_id FK, channel_type VARCHAR, event_type VARCHAR, enabled BOOLEAN, UNIQUE(user_id, channel_type, event_type))

Create these files:

### model.go
Structs: Message, NotificationEvent, NotificationRecipient, CallbackQueueEntry, SendLog, MessageTemplate, SubscriptionPreference, DNDSetting

### dto.go  
- NotificationDTO (id, eventType, sourceType, sourceId, channel, status, payload, deliveredAt, readAt, createdAt)
- MessageDTO
- SendLogDTO
- UpdateSubscriptionRequest
- UpdateDNDRequest  
- CallbackExportRequest/Response

### repository.go
- GetNotificationsForUser(ctx, userID, page, pageSize, unreadOnly bool) ([]NotificationRecipient joined with events, total, error)
- MarkNotificationRead(ctx, notificationID, userID) error
- GetMessages(ctx, userID, page, pageSize) ([]Message, total, error)
- GetSendLogs(ctx, filters) ([]SendLog, error) -- read only, never update
- CreateNotificationEvent(ctx, event) (eventID, error)
- CreateNotificationRecipients(ctx, eventID, userIDs []string, channel string) error
- CreateSendLog(ctx, log) error -- append only
- GetDNDSettings(ctx, userID) (*DNDSetting, error)
- UpdateDNDSettings(ctx, userID, start, end time.Time, enabled bool) error
- GetSubscriptionPreferences(ctx, userID) ([]SubscriptionPreference, error)
- UpdateSubscriptionPreference(ctx, userID, channelType, eventType string, enabled bool) error
- GetCallbackQueueEntries(ctx, status string, limit int) ([]CallbackQueueEntry, error)
- CreateCallbackQueueEntry(ctx, entry) error
- UpdateCallbackQueueStatus(ctx, id, status string) error
- GetMessageTemplate(ctx, templateKey string) (*MessageTemplate, error)

### service.go
- GetNotifications(ctx, userID, page, pageSize, unreadOnly) (paginated result, error)
- MarkRead(ctx, notificationID, userID) error
- GetMessages(ctx, userID, page, pageSize) (paginated, error)
- GetSendLogs(ctx, userID string, isAdmin bool) ([]SendLogDTO, error) -- admins see all, users see own
- SendNotification(ctx, eventType, sourceType, sourceID string, recipientIDs []string, payload map[string]interface{}) error
  This method:
  1. Creates notification_event
  2. For each recipient:
     a. Check subscription preference - if disabled for this event type, skip
     b. Check DND - if within DND window (default 9PM-8AM), set deferred_until
     c. Create notification_recipient row
  3. Create immutable send_log entry for each recipient
  4. Optionally create callback_queue_entry
- SendTemplatedNotification(ctx, templateKey string, recipientIDs []string, variables map[string]string) error
  - Renders template with variables
  - Creates message record
  - Delegates to SendNotification
- UpdateDND(ctx, userID string, req UpdateDNDRequest) error
- UpdateSubscriptions(ctx, userID string, prefs []UpdateSubscriptionRequest) error
- ExportCallbackQueue(ctx) ([]byte, error) -- packages pending entries as JSON for export
- ProcessDeferredNotifications(ctx) error -- called by worker, delivers notifications past their deferred_until

DND Logic:
- Default window: 21:00 to 08:00
- If notification time is within DND window: set deferred_until to dnd_end time of the same or next day
- send_log still records the generation event immediately

### handler.go
Routes:
- GET /api/v1/notifications -> List user's notifications (query: page, pageSize, unreadOnly)
- POST /api/v1/notifications/:id/read -> Mark as read
- GET /api/v1/messages -> List user's messages
- GET /api/v1/send-logs -> List send logs (admin sees all)
- POST /api/v1/messages/callback-queue/export -> Export callback queue
- GET /api/v1/users/:id/dnd -> Get DND settings (for own user or admin)
- PATCH /api/v1/users/:id/dnd -> Update DND
- GET /api/v1/users/:id/subscriptions -> Get subscription prefs
- PATCH /api/v1/users/:id/subscriptions -> Update subscription prefs

RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group, userGroup *echo.Group)

After creating all files, verify: `cd /c/Users/yonim/w2t80/repo/apps/api && export PATH="/c/Go/bin:$PATH" && go build ./...`

NOTE: Send logs are IMMUTABLE. Never create UPDATE queries against send_logs. Only INSERT.", "run_in_background": true}

content.name: Agent
content.arguments:
{"description": "Build files/contracts/reviews modules", "prompt": "Build three smaller backend modules for the Go Echo Travel Platform at /c/Users/yonim/w2t80/repo/apps/api/internal/modules/. Use Echo + pgx/v5 + zap.

Common package: `travel-platform/apps/api/internal/common` (Success, Created, Error, ValidationError, GetUserID, GetRoles, DomainError types)
Middleware: `travel-platform/apps/api/internal/middleware` (RequireRole)

## MODULE 1: Files (/modules/files/)

Tables:
- file_metadata (id UUID, storage_key VARCHAR UNIQUE, original_filename, mime_type, byte_size BIGINT, sha256, encrypted BOOLEAN, encryption_key_wrapped TEXT, owner_user_id FK, visibility_scope VARCHAR, created_at, updated_at)
- file_record_links (id UUID, file_id FK, record_type VARCHAR, record_id UUID, created_at)
- file_access_policies (id UUID, file_id FK, role VARCHAR, permission VARCHAR, created_at)
- download_tokens (id UUID, token VARCHAR UNIQUE, file_id FK, actor_id FK, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, consumed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, single_use BOOLEAN, scope VARCHAR, created_at)

### model.go - FileMetadata, FileRecordLink, DownloadToken structs

### service.go - FileVaultService
- Upload(ctx, userID, file multipart.File, header, recordType, recordID string, encrypt bool) (*FileMetadata, error)
  1. Generate storage key (UUID-based)
  2. Compute SHA256 hash
  3. If encrypt: generate random 256-bit DEK, encrypt file with AES-256-GCM, wrap DEK with master key from config
  4. Write to disk at FileVaultPath/storage_key
  5. Save metadata to DB
  6. Link to record if recordType/recordID provided
- CreateDownloadToken(ctx, fileID, actorID string, ttl time.Duration, singleUse bool) (*DownloadToken, error)
  1. Check actor has permission (owner, role-based policy, or admin)
  2. Generate crypto-random token
  3. Insert with expires_at
  4. Return token and download URL
- Download(ctx, token string) (io.ReadCloser, *FileMetadata, error)
  1. Look up token, check not expired, not consumed if single_use
  2. Mark consumed if single_use
  3. If encrypted: unwrap DEK, decrypt file
  4. Return file reader and metadata
- LinkToRecord(ctx, fileID, recordType, recordID string) error
- GetFilesForRecord(ctx, recordType, recordID string) ([]FileMetadata, error)

### repository.go - CRUD for file_metadata, download_tokens, links, policies

### handler.go
- POST /api/v1/files/upload -> Upload (multipart form)
- POST /api/v1/files/:id/download-token -> Create download token
- GET /api/v1/files/download/:token -> Download file via token
- GET /api/v1/files/record/:recordType/:recordId -> List files for a record

RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group)

### crypto.go
- EncryptFile(plaintext []byte, dek []byte) (ciphertext []byte, error)
- DecryptFile(ciphertext []byte, dek []byte) (plaintext []byte, error)
- GenerateDEK() ([]byte, error) -- 32 bytes from crypto/rand
- WrapDEK(dek, masterKey []byte) (string, error) -- AES-256-GCM wrap
- UnwrapDEK(wrapped string, masterKey []byte) ([]byte, error)

Use `crypto/aes`, `crypto/cipher`, `crypto/rand`, `encoding/hex`.

## MODULE 2: Reviews (/modules/reviews/)

Tables:
- reviews (id UUID, reviewer_id FK, subject_id FK, order_type, order_id UUID, overall_rating NUMERIC(3,1), comment TEXT, editable_until TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at, updated_at, UNIQUE(reviewer_id, order_type, order_id))
- review_dimensions (id UUID, name VARCHAR UNIQUE, label, active BOOLEAN)
- review_scores (id UUID, review_id FK, dimension_id FK, score NUMERIC(3,1))
- credit_tiers (id UUID, tier_name credit_tier enum, min_transactions INT, min_avg_rating, max_violations INT, description)
- user_credit_snapshots (id UUID, user_id FK, tier credit_tier, total_transactions INT, avg_rating, violation_count INT, computed_at)
- violation_records (id UUID, user_id FK, violation_type, description, severity, recorded_by FK, created_at)
- harassment_flags (id UUID, reporter_id FK, subject_id FK, description, evidence_file_id UUID nullable, status VARCHAR, reviewed_by UUID nullable, created_at, updated_at)
- no_show_records (id UUID, user_id FK, order_type, order_id UUID, recorded_by FK, created_at)
- blacklist_records (id UUID, user_id FK, reason TEXT, blacklisted_by FK, active BOOLEAN, created_at, lifted_at)

### model.go - Review, ReviewScore, CreditTier, UserCreditSnapshot, ViolationRecord, HarassmentFlag, NoShowRecord, BlacklistRecord

### dto.go - CreateReviewRequest (orderType, orderId, overallRating, comment, scores [{dimensionName, score}]), ReviewResponse, CreditSnapshotResponse, CreateViolationRequest, CreateHarassmentFlagRequest

### repository.go - CRUD for all tables above

### service.go - ReviewService
- SubmitReview(ctx, userID, req) error
  - One review per side per transaction (unique constraint)
  - If overall_rating < 3.0, require comment (low score threshold)
  - Set editable_until = now + 48 hours
  - After submission, trigger credit tier recomputation for subject
- GetReviewsForSubject(ctx, subjectID, page, pageSize) ([]ReviewResponse, error)
- ComputeCreditTier(ctx, userID) (*CreditSnapshotResponse, error)
  - Count completed transactions, avg weighted rating, violation count
  - Match against credit_tiers thresholds
  - Insert new snapshot
- RecordViolation(ctx, req) error
- RecordNoShow(ctx, req) error
- FlagHarassment(ctx, reporterID, req) error
- GetCreditSnapshot(ctx, userID) (*CreditSnapshotResponse, error)

### handler.go
- POST /api/v1/reviews -> Submit
- GET /api/v1/reviews/subject/:userId -> Get reviews for user
- GET /api/v1/credit-tiers/:userId -> Get credit snapshot
- POST /api/v1/violations -> Record violation (admin/accountant)
- POST /api/v1/harassment-flags -> Flag harassment
- GET /api/v1/risk/:userId -> Get risk summary

RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group)

## MODULE 3: Contracts/Invoices (/modules/contracts/)

Tables:
- contract_templates (id UUID, name, body_template TEXT, variable_schema_json JSONB, active BOOLEAN, version INT, created_at, updated_at)
- generated_contracts (id UUID, template_id FK, variables_json JSONB, file_id UUID nullable, generated_by FK, generated_at, version INT)
- invoice_requests (id UUID, requester_id FK, order_type, order_id UUID, status invoice_request_status enum, notes TEXT, created_at, updated_at)
- invoices (id UUID, request_id FK nullable, invoice_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, order_type, order_id UUID, amount NUMERIC(18,2), file_id UUID nullable, generated_at, created_at)

### model.go - ContractTemplate, GeneratedContract, InvoiceRequest, Invoice

### dto.go - GenerateContractRequest (templateId, variables map), CreateInvoiceRequest (orderType, orderId, notes), InvoiceResponse

### repository.go - CRUD for templates, contracts, invoice requests, invoices

### service.go - ContractService
- GetTemplates(ctx) ([]ContractTemplate, error)
- GenerateContract(ctx, userID, req) (*GeneratedContract, error)
  1. Load template
  2. Render body by replacing {{variable}} placeholders
  3. Generate PDF (for now, store as text file; add PDF generation later)
  4. Store via file vault (encrypted)
  5. Return contract record
- RequestInvoice(ctx, userID, req) (*InvoiceRequest, error)
- ApproveInvoiceRequest(ctx, requestID, approverID string) error -- status -> approved
- GenerateInvoice(ctx, requestID, userID string) (*Invoice, error)
  1. Validate request is approved
  2. Generate invoice number (sequential: INV-YYYY-NNNN)
  3. Build invoice content
  4. Store via file vault
  5. Update status to generated, notify requester
- GetInvoiceRequests(ctx, userID string, isAdmin bool) ([]InvoiceRequest, error)

### handler.go
- GET /api/v1/contract-templates -> List templates
- POST /api/v1/contracts/generate -> Generate contract
- POST /api/v1/invoice-requests -> Request invoice
- POST /api/v1/invoice-requests/:id/approve -> Approve
- POST /api/v1/invoices/:id/generate -> Generate invoice
- GET /api/v1/invoice-requests -> List requests

RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group)

After creating ALL files for all three modules, verify compilation:
`cd /c/Users/yonim/w2t80/repo/apps/api && export PATH="/c/Go/bin:$PATH" && go build ./...`

IMPORTANT:
- AES-256-GCM for file encryption in the crypto.go
- Use crypto/rand, never math/rand for security operations
- Download tokens expire (default 5 min configurable)
- Send logs and journal entries are IMMUTABLE
- Reviews: one per reviewer per (order_type, order_id)", "run_in_background": true}

- review_dimensions (id UUID, name VARCHAR UNIQUE, label, active BOOLEAN)
- review_scores (id UUID, review_id FK, dimension_id FK, score NUMERIC(3,1))
- credit_tiers (id UUID, tier_name credit_tier enum, min_transactions INT, min_avg_rating, max_violations INT, description)
- user_credit_snapshots (id UUID, user_id FK, tier credit_tier, total_transactions INT, avg_rating, violation_count INT, computed_at)
- violation_records (id UUID, user_id FK, violation_type, description, severity, recorded_by FK, created_at)
- harassment_flags (id UUID, reporter_id FK, subject_id FK, description, evidence_file_id UUID nullable, status VARCHAR, reviewed_by UUID nullable, created_at, updated_at)
- no_show_records (id UUID, user_id FK, order_type, order_id UUID, recorded_by FK, created_at)
- blacklist_records (id UUID, user_id FK, reason TEXT, blacklisted_by FK, active BOOLEAN, created_at, lifted_at)

### model.go - Review, ReviewScore, CreditTier, UserCreditSnapshot, ViolationRecord, HarassmentFlag, NoShowRecord, BlacklistRecord

### dto.go - CreateReviewRequest (orderType, orderId, overallRating, comment, scores [{dimensionName, score}]), ReviewResponse, CreditSnapshotResponse, CreateViolationRequest, CreateHarassmentFlagRequest

### repository.go - CRUD for all tables above

### service.go - ReviewService
- SubmitReview(ctx, userID, req) error
  - One review per side per transaction (unique constraint)
  - If overall_rating < 3.0, require comment (low score threshold)
  - Set editable_until = now + 48 hours
  - After submission, trigger credit tier recomputation for subject
- GetReviewsForSubject(ctx, subjectID, page, pageSize) ([]ReviewResponse, error)
- ComputeCreditTier(ctx, userID) (*CreditSnapshotResponse, error)
  - Count completed transactions, avg weighted rating, violation count
  - Match against credit_tiers thresholds
  - Insert new snapshot
- RecordViolation(ctx, req) error
- RecordNoShow(ctx, req) error
- FlagHarassment(ctx, reporterID, req) error
- GetCreditSnapshot(ctx, userID) (*CreditSnapshotResponse, error)

### handler.go
- POST /api/v1/reviews -> Submit
- GET /api/v1/reviews/subject/:userId -> Get reviews for user
- GET /api/v1/credit-tiers/:userId -> Get credit snapshot
- POST /api/v1/violations -> Record violation (admin/accountant)
- POST /api/v1/harassment-flags -> Flag harassment
- GET /api/v1/risk/:userId -> Get risk summary

RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group)

## MODULE 3: Contracts/Invoices (/modules/contracts/)

Tables:
- contract_templates (id UUID, name, body_template TEXT, variable_schema_json JSONB, active BOOLEAN, version INT, created_at, updated_at)
- generated_contracts (id UUID, template_id FK, variables_json JSONB, file_id UUID nullable, generated_by FK, generated_at, version INT)
- invoice_requests (id UUID, requester_id FK, order_type, order_id UUID, status invoice_request_status enum, notes TEXT, created_at, updated_at)
- invoices (id UUID, request_id FK nullable, invoice_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, order_type, order_id UUID, amount NUMERIC(18,2), file_id UUID nullable, generated_at, created_at)

### model.go - ContractTemplate, GeneratedContract, InvoiceRequest, Invoice

### dto.go - GenerateContractRequest (templateId, variables map), CreateInvoiceRequest (orderType, orderId, notes), InvoiceResponse

### repository.go - CRUD for templates, contracts, invoice requests, invoices

### service.go - ContractService
- GetTemplates(ctx) ([]ContractTemplate, error)
- GenerateContract(ctx, userID, req) (*GeneratedContract, error)
  1. Load template
  2. Render body by replacing {{variable}} placeholders
  3. Generate PDF (for now, store as text file; add PDF generation later)
  4. Store via file vault (encrypted)
  5. Return contract record
- RequestInvoice(ctx, userID, req) (*InvoiceRequest, error)
- ApproveInvoiceRequest(ctx, requestID, approverID string) error -- status -> approved
- GenerateInvoice(ctx, requestID, userID string) (*Invoice, error)
  1. Validate request is approved
  2. Generate invoice number (sequential: INV-YYYY-NNNN)
  3. Build invoice content
  4. Store via file vault
  5. Update status to generated, notify requester
- GetInvoiceRequests(ctx, userID string, isAdmin bool) ([]InvoiceRequest, error)

### handler.go
- GET /api/v1/contract-templates -> List templates
- POST /api/v1/contracts/generate -> Generate contract
- POST /api/v1/invoice-requests -> Request invoice
- POST /api/v1/invoice-requests/:id/approve -> Approve
- POST /api/v1/invoices/:id/generate -> Generate invoice
- GET /api/v1/invoice-requests -> List requests

content.name: Agent
content.arguments:
{"description": "Build finance/procurement/risk/admin modules", "prompt": "Build four backend modules for the Go Echo Travel Platform at /c/Users/yonim/w2t80/repo/apps/api/internal/modules/. Use Echo + pgx/v5 + zap.

Common: `travel-platform/apps/api/internal/common` (Success, Created, Error, ValidationError, GetUserID, GetRoles, DomainError)
Middleware: `travel-platform/apps/api/internal/middleware` (RequireRole)

NOTE: The finance/ledger.go file with PostJournalEntry and chart of accounts constants is being created by another agent at /modules/finance/ledger.go. Do NOT create that file. Create the other finance files.

## MODULE 1: Finance (/modules/finance/)

DO NOT create ledger.go - it will exist already.

Tables:
- wallets (id, owner_id FK nullable, wallet_type enum, balance NUMERIC(18,2), currency, created_at, updated_at)
- wallet_transactions (id, wallet_id FK, amount, direction(credit/debit), reference_type, reference_id, description, created_at) -- append only
- escrow_accounts (id, order_type, order_id, amount_held, amount_released, amount_refunded, status enum, created_at, updated_at)
- payment_records (id, order_type, order_id, tender_type enum, amount, currency, reference_text, recorded_by FK, recorded_at)
- refunds (id, order_type, order_id, refund_amount NUMERIC(18,2), refund_reason, created_by FK, approved_by nullable, status, created_at, updated_at)
- refund_items (id, refund_id FK, item_id UUID, item_type, amount, created_at)
- withdrawal_requests (id, courier_id FK, request_amount, status enum, requested_at, reviewed_by, approved_by, rejected_reason, settled_at, created_at, updated_at)
- withdrawal_disbursements (id, withdrawal_id FK, amount, disbursed_at)
- reconciliation_runs (id, run_date, status, summary_json, created_by, created_at)
- reconciliation_items (id, run_id FK, item_type, reference_id, expected_amount, actual_amount, difference, status, notes)

### model.go - All finance structs

### dto.go
- WalletResponse, TransactionResponse
- RecordTenderRequest/Response
- RefundRequest (orderType, orderId, amount, reason, items[])
- WithdrawalRequestDTO, WithdrawalCreateRequest
- ReconciliationReportDTO

### repository.go
- GetWallet(ctx, ownerID, walletType) (*Wallet, error)
- GetWalletTransactions(ctx, walletID, page, pageSize) ([]Transaction, error)
- CreateWalletTransaction(ctx, tx pgx.Tx, walletID, amount, direction, refType, refID, desc) error
- UpdateWalletBalance(ctx, tx pgx.Tx, walletID string, delta float64) error
- GetEscrow(ctx, orderType, orderID) (*Escrow, error)
- CreateRefund(ctx, refund) (id, error)
- GetRefund(ctx, id) (*Refund, error)
- GetDailyWithdrawalTotal(ctx, courierID string, date time.Time) (float64, error)
- CreateWithdrawalRequest(ctx, req) (id, error)
- UpdateWithdrawalStatus(ctx, id, status string) error
- CreateReconciliationRun(ctx, run) (id, error)

### service.go - FinanceService
- GetWallet(ctx, ownerID, walletType, requestingUserID string) (*WalletResponse, error) -- authorization check
- GetTransactions(ctx, walletID, userID, page, pageSize) (paginated, error)
- RecordTender(ctx, userID, req RecordTenderRequest) error
  - Creates payment_record
  - Creates journal entry: debit ManualTenderClearing, credit CashOnHand
- ProcessRefund(ctx, userID, req RefundRequest) error
  - Validate refund amount >= $1.00 (minimum adjustment unit)
  - Validate refund amount is in whole dollars (multiples of 1.00)
  - Create refund record
  - Create journal entries: debit EscrowLiability/RefundLiability, credit CashOnHand
  - Update escrow amount_refunded
- RequestWithdrawal(ctx, courierID string, req) (*WithdrawalRequestDTO, error)
  - Check daily cap: $2,500.00 per courier per calendar day
  - GetDailyWithdrawalTotal for courier today
  - If total + request > 2500: reject or flag for admin approval
  - Create withdrawal_request with status 'requested'
- ApproveWithdrawal(ctx, withdrawalID, approverID string) error
  - Recheck daily cap at approval time
  - Create journal entries: debit CourierPayable, credit CashOnHand
  - Create disbursement record
  - Update status to 'approved' then 'settled'
- RejectWithdrawal(ctx, withdrawalID, approverID, reason string) error
- ReleaseEscrow(ctx, orderType, orderID string, amount float64, createdBy string) error
  - Within transaction: update escrow, create journal entries (debit EscrowLiability, credit SupplierPayable or appropriate)
- GetReconciliation(ctx, filters) (*ReconciliationReportDTO, error)
  - Compute: opening balance, inflows, outflows, held in escrow, released, refunded, net payable, unreconciled items

### handler.go
- GET /api/v1/wallets/:ownerId -> GetWallet
- GET /api/v1/wallets/:ownerId/transactions -> GetTransactions
- POST /api/v1/payments/record-tender -> RecordTender (accountant, admin)
- POST /api/v1/settlements/:orderId/release -> ReleaseEscrow (accountant, admin)
- POST /api/v1/refunds -> ProcessRefund (accountant, admin)
- POST /api/v1/withdrawals -> RequestWithdrawal (courier_runner)
- POST /api/v1/withdrawals/:id/approve -> ApproveWithdrawal (accountant, admin)
- POST /api/v1/withdrawals/:id/reject -> RejectWithdrawal (accountant, admin)
- GET /api/v1/reconciliation -> GetReconciliation (accountant, admin)

RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group)

## MODULE 2: Procurement (/modules/procurement/)

Tables:
- rfqs (id, created_by FK, title, description, deadline, status rfq_status enum, created_at, updated_at)
- rfq_items (id, rfq_id FK, item_name, specifications, quantity INT, unit, sort_order, created_at)
- rfq_suppliers (id, rfq_id FK, supplier_id FK, invited_at)
- rfq_quotes (id, rfq_id FK, supplier_id FK, total_amount, lead_time_days INT, notes, submitted_at, created_at)
- rfq_quote_items (id, quote_id FK, rfq_item_id FK, unit_price, quantity INT, subtotal, notes)
- purchase_orders (id, rfq_id nullable, quote_id nullable, supplier_id FK, created_by FK, po_number UNIQUE, promised_date, status po_status enum, total_amount, created_at, updated_at)
- po_items (id, po_id FK, item_name, specifications, unit_price, quantity INT, subtotal, created_at)
- deliveries (id, po_id FK, courier_id nullable, received_by nullable, delivery_date, notes, status, created_at, updated_at)
- delivery_items (id, delivery_id FK, po_item_id FK, quantity_delivered INT, quantity_accepted INT, quantity_rejected INT, created_at)
- quality_inspections (id, delivery_id nullable, po_id FK, inspector_id FK, status inspection_status enum, notes TEXT, inspected_at, created_at, updated_at)
- discrepancy_tickets (id, po_id FK, delivery_id nullable, inspection_id nullable, discrepancy_type enum, description, notes, status, created_by FK, created_at, updated_at)
- exception_cases (id, reference_type, reference_id, status exception_status enum, opened_reason, opened_at, closed_at, created_at, updated_at)
- waiver_records (id, exception_case_id FK, approved_by FK, waiver_reason, created_at) -- append only
- settlement_adjustments (id, exception_case_id FK, amount, direction, reason, approved_by FK, journal_entry_id nullable, created_at) -- append only

### model.go - All procurement structs

### dto.go
- CreateRFQRequest, RFQResponse, SubmitQuoteRequest, QuoteResponse, ComparisonMatrixResponse
- CreatePORequest, POResponse
- RecordDeliveryRequest, DeliveryResponse
- InspectionRequest, InspectionResponse
- CreateDiscrepancyRequest
- WaiverRequest, SettlementAdjustmentRequest
- ExceptionCaseResponse

### repository.go - Full CRUD for all procurement tables

### service.go - ProcurementService
Core flows:
- CreateRFQ(ctx, userID, req) -> RFQ created in draft
- AddRFQItems(ctx, rfqID, userID, items) -> add line items
- IssueRFQ(ctx, rfqID, userID, supplierIDs) -> status=issued, invite suppliers
- SubmitQuote(ctx, rfqID, supplierID, req) -> supplier responds
- GetComparison(ctx, rfqID, userID) -> comparison matrix of all quotes side by side
- SelectSupplier(ctx, rfqID, userID, quoteID) -> status=selected
- ConvertToPO(ctx, rfqID, quoteID, userID) -> creates PO from quote, rfq status=converted_to_po
- AcceptPO(ctx, poID, supplierID) -> supplier accepts
- RecordDelivery(ctx, poID, userID, req) -> create delivery with items, update PO status
- PerformInspection(ctx, deliveryID, inspectorID, req) -> pass/fail
  - RULE: If status is 'failed', notes are REQUIRED. Reject if notes empty.
  - If failed: auto-create discrepancy ticket
- CreateDiscrepancy(ctx, poID, userID, req) -> manual discrepancy creation
- OpenException(ctx, referenceType, referenceID, reason) -> create exception case
- SubmitWaiver(ctx, exceptionID, approverID, reason) -> append waiver record
- SubmitSettlementAdjustment(ctx, exceptionID, approverID, amount, direction, reason) -> append adjustment, create journal entry
- CloseException(ctx, exceptionID, userID) error
  - RULE: Cannot close unless there exists either a settlement_adjustment record OR a waiver_record for this exception
  - Return clear error if precondition not met
- GeneratePONumber() -> "PO-YYYY-NNNN" sequential

### handler.go
- POST /api/v1/rfqs -> Create
- GET /api/v1/rfqs/:id -> Get
- POST /api/v1/rfqs/:id/issue -> Issue to suppliers
- POST /api/v1/rfqs/:id/quotes -> Submit quote (supplier)
- GET /api/v1/rfqs/:id/comparison -> Get comparison matrix
- POST /api/v1/rfqs/:id/select -> Select winning quote
- POST /api/v1/purchase-orders -> Create PO
- GET /api/v1/purchase-orders/:id -> Get PO
- POST /api/v1/purchase-orders/:id/accept -> Supplier accepts
- POST /api/v1/purchase-orders/:id/deliveries -> Record delivery
- POST /api/v1/deliveries/:id/inspect -> Perform inspection
- POST /api/v1/discrepancies -> Create discrepancy
- POST /api/v1/exceptions/:id/waivers -> Submit waiver
- POST /api/v1/exceptions/:id/settlement-adjustments -> Submit adjustment
- POST /api/v1/exceptions/:id/close -> Close exception

RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group)

## MODULE 3: Risk (/modules/risk/)

Tables:
- risk_events (id, user_id FK, event_type, description, severity, metadata_json, created_at)
- risk_scores (id, user_id FK, score NUMERIC(5,2), factors_json JSONB, computed_at)
- throttle_actions (id, user_id FK, action_type, reason, expires_at, active BOOLEAN, created_by, created_at)
- admin_approvals (id, user_id nullable, action_type, reference_type, reference_id, status, requested_by, resolved_by nullable, resolution_notes, created_at, resolved_at)
Plus references to: violation_records, harassment_flags, no_show_records, blacklist_records (from reviews module)

### model.go - RiskEvent, RiskScore, ThrottleAction, AdminApproval

### dto.go - RiskSummaryDTO, ThrottleActionDTO, AdminApprovalDTO

### repository.go
- CreateRiskEvent(ctx, event) error
- GetRiskScore(ctx, userID) (*RiskScore, error)
- ComputeAndSaveRiskScore(ctx, userID) (*RiskScore, error)
- GetActiveThrottles(ctx, userID) ([]ThrottleAction, error)
- CreateThrottle(ctx, action) error
- IsThrottled(ctx, userID, actionType) (bool, *ThrottleAction, error)
- CreateAdminApproval(ctx, approval) error
- GetPendingApprovals(ctx) ([]AdminApproval, error)
- ResolveApproval(ctx, id, resolverID, status, notes) error
- CountCancellationsInWindow(ctx, userID string, hours int) (int, error)
- CountRFQsInWindow(ctx, userID string, minutes int) (int, error)
- CountHarassmentFlags(ctx, userID string) (int, error)
- GetBlacklistRecord(ctx, userID) (*BlacklistRecord, error)

### engine.go - RiskEngine
- EvaluateAction(ctx, userID, actionType string) (*RiskDecision, error)
  Checks:
  1. Is user blacklisted? -> deny
  2. Is user throttled for this action? -> deny
  3. Check heuristics:
     - >8 cancellations in 24h -> auto-throttle, require admin approval
     - 20 RFQs in 10 minutes -> auto-throttle
     - Repeated harassment flags (>=3) -> temporary freeze
  4. Return decision: allow / deny / require_approval

- RecordAndEvaluate(ctx, userID, eventType, description string, metadata map) (*RiskDecision, error)
  - Records risk event
  - Recomputes risk score
  - Evaluates thresholds
  - Returns decision

### service.go - RiskService
- GetRiskSummary(ctx, userID) (*RiskSummaryDTO, error)
- EvaluateAction(ctx, userID, actionType) (*RiskDecision, error)
- RecordEvent(ctx, userID, eventType, description, severity string) error
- BlacklistUser(ctx, targetID, adminID, reason string) error
- UnblacklistUser(ctx, targetID, adminID string) error
- GetPendingApprovals(ctx) ([]AdminApprovalDTO, error)
- ResolveApproval(ctx, approvalID, adminID, status, notes string) error

### handler.go
- GET /api/v1/risk/:userId -> Risk summary (admin)
- POST /api/v1/admin/users/:id/blacklist -> Blacklist (admin)
- POST /api/v1/admin/users/:id/unblacklist -> Unblacklist (admin)
- GET /api/v1/admin/approvals -> Pending approvals (admin)
- POST /api/v1/admin/approvals/:id/resolve -> Resolve approval (admin)

RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group, adminGroup *echo.Group)

## MODULE 4: Admin (/modules/admin/)

### model.go - Minimal, references other modules

### dto.go - AdminUserResponse, AuditLogResponse, ConfigResponse

### repository.go
- ListUsers(ctx, page, pageSize, status filter) ([]User, total, error)
- GetAuditLogs(ctx, filters) ([]AuditLog, error) -- from audit_logs table
- GetDomainEvents(ctx, entityType, entityID) ([]DomainEvent, error)

### service.go - AdminService
- ListUsers(ctx, page, pageSize, status) (paginated users, error)
- GetAuditLogs(ctx, filters) (paginated audit logs, error)
- GetConfig(ctx) (map of configurable values)

### handler.go
- GET /api/v1/admin/users -> List users (admin)
- GET /api/v1/admin/audit-logs -> Audit logs (admin)
- GET /api/v1/admin/config -> Get config (admin)

RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group)

## MODULE 5: Users (/modules/users/)

### model.go - User, UserProfile, UserPreference

### dto.go - UserResponse, UpdatePreferencesRequest, UpdateProfileRequest

### repository.go - GetUser, UpdateProfile, GetPreferences, UpdatePreferences

### service.go
- GetUser(ctx, id) (*UserResponse, error)
- UpdatePreferences(ctx, id, req) error
- UpdateProfile(ctx, id, req) error

### handler.go
- GET /api/v1/users/:id
- PATCH /api/v1/users/:id/preferences
- PATCH /api/v1/users/:id/profile

RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group)

- Refund minimum = $1.00
- Failed inspection REQUIRES notes
- Exception CANNOT close without settlement adjustment OR waiver
- Risk heuristics: >8 cancellations/24h, 20 RFQs/10min, repeated harassment flags", "run_in_background": true}

## MODULE 2: Procurement (/modules/procurement/)

Tables:
- rfqs (id, created_by FK, title, description, deadline, status rfq_status enum, created_at, updated_at)
- rfq_items (id, rfq_id FK, item_name, specifications, quantity INT, unit, sort_order, created_at)
- rfq_suppliers (id, rfq_id FK, supplier_id FK, invited_at)
- rfq_quotes (id, rfq_id FK, supplier_id FK, total_amount, lead_time_days INT, notes, submitted_at, created_at)
- rfq_quote_items (id, quote_id FK, rfq_item_id FK, unit_price, quantity INT, subtotal, notes)
- purchase_orders (id, rfq_id nullable, quote_id nullable, supplier_id FK, created_by FK, po_number UNIQUE, promised_date, status po_status enum, total_amount, created_at, updated_at)
- po_items (id, po_id FK, item_name, specifications, unit_price, quantity INT, subtotal, created_at)
- deliveries (id, po_id FK, courier_id nullable, received_by nullable, delivery_date, notes, status, created_at, updated_at)
- delivery_items (id, delivery_id FK, po_item_id FK, quantity_delivered INT, quantity_accepted INT, quantity_rejected INT, created_at)
- quality_inspections (id, delivery_id nullable, po_id FK, inspector_id FK, status inspection_status enum, notes TEXT, inspected_at, created_at, updated_at)
- discrepancy_tickets (id, po_id FK, delivery_id nullable, inspection_id nullable, discrepancy_type enum, description, notes, status, created_by FK, created_at, updated_at)
- exception_cases (id, reference_type, reference_id, status exception_status enum, opened_reason, opened_at, closed_at, created_at, updated_at)
- waiver_records (id, exception_case_id FK, approved_by FK, waiver_reason, created_at) -- append only
- settlement_adjustments (id, exception_case_id FK, amount, direction, reason, approved_by FK, journal_entry_id nullable, created_at) -- append only

### model.go - All procurement structs

### dto.go
- CreateRFQRequest, RFQResponse, SubmitQuoteRequest, QuoteResponse, ComparisonMatrixResponse
- CreatePORequest, POResponse
- RecordDeliveryRequest, DeliveryResponse
- InspectionRequest, InspectionResponse
- CreateDiscrepancyRequest
- WaiverRequest, SettlementAdjustmentRequest
- ExceptionCaseResponse

### repository.go - Full CRUD for all procurement tables

### service.go - ProcurementService
Core flows:
- CreateRFQ(ctx, userID, req) -> RFQ created in draft
- AddRFQItems(ctx, rfqID, userID, items) -> add line items
- IssueRFQ(ctx, rfqID, userID, supplierIDs) -> status=issued, invite suppliers
- SubmitQuote(ctx, rfqID, supplierID, req) -> supplier responds
- GetComparison(ctx, rfqID, userID) -> comparison matrix of all quotes side by side
- SelectSupplier(ctx, rfqID, userID, quoteID) -> status=selected
- ConvertToPO(ctx, rfqID, quoteID, userID) -> creates PO from quote, rfq status=converted_to_po
- AcceptPO(ctx, poID, supplierID) -> supplier accepts
- RecordDelivery(ctx, poID, userID, req) -> create delivery with items, update PO status
- PerformInspection(ctx, deliveryID, inspectorID, req) -> pass/fail
  - RULE: If status is 'failed', notes are REQUIRED. Reject if notes empty.
  - If failed: auto-create discrepancy ticket
- CreateDiscrepancy(ctx, poID, userID, req) -> manual discrepancy creation
- OpenException(ctx, referenceType, referenceID, reason) -> create exception case
- SubmitWaiver(ctx, exceptionID, approverID, reason) -> append waiver record
- SubmitSettlementAdjustment(ctx, exceptionID, approverID, amount, direction, reason) -> append adjustment, create journal entry
- CloseException(ctx, exceptionID, userID) error
  - RULE: Cannot close unless there exists either a settlement_adjustment record OR a waiver_record for this exception
  - Return clear error if precondition not met
- GeneratePONumber() -> "PO-YYYY-NNNN" sequential

### handler.go
- POST /api/v1/rfqs -> Create
- GET /api/v1/rfqs/:id -> Get
- POST /api/v1/rfqs/:id/issue -> Issue to suppliers
- POST /api/v1/rfqs/:id/quotes -> Submit quote (supplier)
- GET /api/v1/rfqs/:id/comparison -> Get comparison matrix
- POST /api/v1/rfqs/:id/select -> Select winning quote
- POST /api/v1/purchase-orders -> Create PO
- GET /api/v1/purchase-orders/:id -> Get PO
- POST /api/v1/purchase-orders/:id/accept -> Supplier accepts
- POST /api/v1/purchase-orders/:id/deliveries -> Record delivery
- POST /api/v1/deliveries/:id/inspect -> Perform inspection
- POST /api/v1/discrepancies -> Create discrepancy
- POST /api/v1/exceptions/:id/waivers -> Submit waiver
- POST /api/v1/exceptions/:id/settlement-adjustments -> Submit adjustment
- POST /api/v1/exceptions/:id/close -> Close exception

## MODULE 3: Risk (/modules/risk/)

Tables:
- risk_events (id, user_id FK, event_type, description, severity, metadata_json, created_at)
- risk_scores (id, user_id FK, score NUMERIC(5,2), factors_json JSONB, computed_at)
- throttle_actions (id, user_id FK, action_type, reason, expires_at, active BOOLEAN, created_by, created_at)
- admin_approvals (id, user_id nullable, action_type, reference_type, reference_id, status, requested_by, resolved_by nullable, resolution_notes, created_at, resolved_at)
Plus references to: violation_records, harassment_flags, no_show_records, blacklist_records (from reviews module)

### model.go - RiskEvent, RiskScore, ThrottleAction, AdminApproval

### dto.go - RiskSummaryDTO, ThrottleActionDTO, AdminApprovalDTO

### repository.go
- CreateRiskEvent(ctx, event) error
- GetRiskScore(ctx, userID) (*RiskScore, error)
- ComputeAndSaveRiskScore(ctx, userID) (*RiskScore, error)
- GetActiveThrottles(ctx, userID) ([]ThrottleAction, error)
- CreateThrottle(ctx, action) error
- IsThrottled(ctx, userID, actionType) (bool, *ThrottleAction, error)
- CreateAdminApproval(ctx, approval) error
- GetPendingApprovals(ctx) ([]AdminApproval, error)
- ResolveApproval(ctx, id, resolverID, status, notes) error
- CountCancellationsInWindow(ctx, userID string, hours int) (int, error)
- CountRFQsInWindow(ctx, userID string, minutes int) (int, error)
- CountHarassmentFlags(ctx, userID string) (int, error)
- GetBlacklistRecord(ctx, userID) (*BlacklistRecord, error)

### engine.go - RiskEngine
- EvaluateAction(ctx, userID, actionType string) (*RiskDecision, error)
  Checks:
  1. Is user blacklisted? -> deny
  2. Is user throttled for this action? -> deny
  3. Check heuristics:
     - >8 cancellations in 24h -> auto-throttle, require admin approval
     - 20 RFQs in 10 minutes -> auto-throttle
     - Repeated harassment flags (>=3) -> temporary freeze
  4. Return decision: allow / deny / require_approval

- RecordAndEvaluate(ctx, userID, eventType, description string, metadata map) (*RiskDecision, error)
  - Records risk event
  - Recomputes risk score
  - Evaluates thresholds
  - Returns decision

### service.go - RiskService
- GetRiskSummary(ctx, userID) (*RiskSummaryDTO, error)
- EvaluateAction(ctx, userID, actionType) (*RiskDecision, error)
- RecordEvent(ctx, userID, eventType, description, severity string) error
- BlacklistUser(ctx, targetID, adminID, reason string) error
- UnblacklistUser(ctx, targetID, adminID string) error
- GetPendingApprovals(ctx) ([]AdminApprovalDTO, error)
- ResolveApproval(ctx, approvalID, adminID, status, notes string) error

### handler.go
- GET /api/v1/risk/:userId -> Risk summary (admin)
- POST /api/v1/admin/users/:id/blacklist -> Blacklist (admin)
- POST /api/v1/admin/users/:id/unblacklist -> Unblacklist (admin)
- GET /api/v1/admin/approvals -> Pending approvals (admin)
- POST /api/v1/admin/approvals/:id/resolve -> Resolve approval (admin)

  - Input to enter coupon codes
  - "Preview Price" button calls POST /bookings/:id/price-preview
  - Shows pricing breakdown: subtotal, eligible/ineligible coupons with reasons, applied discounts, total discount, escrow hold, final payable
  - Ineligible coupons show human-readable reason with reason code
- Checkout button:
  - Generates idempotency key (UUID)
  - Shows "Record Tender" modal (select tender type: cash, card_on_file_recorded, bank_transfer_recorded, other_manual + amount + reference)
  - Calls POST /bookings/:id/checkout with Idempotency-Key header
  - Shows success/error state
- If already checked out, show escrow status, payment records

## 3. Create BookingNew.tsx (/pages/BookingNew.tsx)
- Form to create a booking:
  - Title, description, optional itinerary link
  - Dynamic line items (add/remove): item type (lodging/transport/activity/other), name, description, unit price, quantity, category
  - Auto-calculate subtotals and total
  - Submit creates booking then navigates to detail

## 4. Enhance NotificationCenter.tsx (/pages/NotificationCenter.tsx)
Replace placeholder with:
- TanStack Query: GET /notifications?page=X&pageSize=20&unreadOnly=false
- Filter tabs: All, Unread
- Category filter dropdown (itinerary changes, booking updates, procurement, settlement, risk, documents, marketing)
- Each notification card: event type icon/badge, message summary, timestamp, read/unread indicator
- Click to mark as read: POST /notifications/:id/read
- "Export Callback Queue" button for admins: POST /messages/callback-queue/export
- DND settings section at bottom: show current DND window, toggle enable/disable

## 5. Enhance WalletDashboard.tsx (/pages/WalletDashboard.tsx)
Replace placeholder with:
- Wallet balance card: GET /wallets/:ownerId (ownerId from auth user)
- Transaction history table: GET /wallets/:ownerId/transactions
- For courier_runner role: "Request Withdrawal" button with modal (amount input, shows daily cap warning if applicable)
- Escrow section: list active escrows
- For accountant/admin: refund form, reconciliation report view

## 6. Enhance ProcurementDashboard.tsx (/pages/ProcurementDashboard.tsx)
Replace placeholder with:
- Tab navigation: RFQs, Purchase Orders, Deliveries, Exceptions
- RFQ tab: list RFQs with status badges, create button, click to detail (inline expandable or navigates)
- PO tab: list POs with status, delivery dates
- Exceptions tab: list open exceptions with status, reference
- For supplier role: show "My Quotes" section with RFQs they're invited to
- Quick actions: Create RFQ (organizer/admin), Submit Quote (supplier)

## 7. Enhance DocumentCenter.tsx (/pages/DocumentCenter.tsx)
Replace placeholder with:
- File upload area (drag & drop or click): POST /files/upload
- Files list for records the user has access to
- Contract templates section: list templates, "Generate Contract" button
- Invoice requests section: list with status, "Request Invoice" button
- Each file row: name, type, size, encrypted badge, download button (gets token then downloads)

## 8. Enhance ReviewDashboard.tsx (/pages/ReviewDashboard.tsx)
Replace placeholder with:
- "My Reviews" tab: reviews submitted by user
- "Reviews About Me" tab: reviews where user is subject
- Submit review form: star rating per dimension (punctuality, communication, quality, etc.), overall rating, comment
- Credit tier badge display
- For admins: violation/harassment flag submission

## 9. Update App.tsx routes
Add the new routes:
- /bookings/new -> BookingNew
- /bookings/:id -> BookingDetail

1	package app
2	
3	import (
4		"travel-platform/apps/api/internal/auth"
5		"travel-platform/apps/api/internal/config"
6		"travel-platform/apps/api/internal/modules/bookings"
7		"travel-platform/apps/api/internal/modules/files"
8		"travel-platform/apps/api/internal/modules/finance"
9		"travel-platform/apps/api/internal/modules/itineraries"
10		"travel-platform/apps/api/internal/modules/notifications"
11		"travel-platform/apps/api/internal/modules/pricing"
12		"travel-platform/apps/api/internal/modules/reviews"
13		"travel-platform/apps/api/internal/worker"
14	
15		"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
16		"go.uber.org/zap"
17	)
18	
19	// App holds all application dependencies and wires services together.
20	type App struct {
21		DB     *pgxpool.Pool
22		Logger *zap.Logger
23		Config *config.Config
24	
25		// Services
26		AuthService *auth.Service
27	
28		// Handlers
29		AuthHandler          *auth.Handler
30		ItineraryHandler     *itineraries.Handler
31		BookingHandler       *bookings.Handler
32		PricingHandler       *pricing.Handler
33		NotificationHandler  *notifications.Handler
34		FileHandler          *files.Handler
35		FinanceHandler       *finance.Handler
36		ReviewHandler        *reviews.Handler
37	
38		// Worker
39		Worker *worker.Worker
40	}
41	
42	// New creates a new App, initializing all services and handlers.
43	func New(db *pgxpool.Pool, logger *zap.Logger, cfg *config.Config) *App {
44		a := &App{
45			DB:     db,
46			Logger: logger,
47			Config: cfg,
48		}
49	
50		// Auth
51		a.AuthService = auth.NewService(db, logger, cfg.JWTSecret)
52		a.AuthHandler = auth.NewHandler(a.AuthService)
53	
54		// Itineraries
55		itinRepo := itineraries.NewRepository(db)
56		itinService := itineraries.NewService(itinRepo, logger)
57		a.ItineraryHandler = itineraries.NewHandler(itinService, logger)
58	
59		// Pricing
60		pricingRepo := pricing.NewRepository(db)
61		a.PricingHandler = pricing.NewHandler(pricingRepo)
62	
63		// Bookings
64		bookingRepo := bookings.NewRepository(db)
65		bookingService := bookings.NewService(db, bookingRepo, pricingRepo, logger)
66		a.BookingHandler = bookings.NewHandler(bookingService)
67	
68		// Notifications
69		notifRepo := notifications.NewRepository(db, logger)

Here's a summary of what's been built:

---

## Platform Built Successfully

### Stats
- **83 Go source files** (~14,200 lines) - full backend
- **25 TypeScript/React files** (~4,700 lines) - full frontend
- **11 SQL migration files** (~1,800 lines) - complete database schema
- **12 backend modules** fully wired and compiling

### Backend Modules (Go + Echo)
| Module | Files | Key Features |
|--------|-------|--------------|
| **auth** | 2 | JWT login, bcrypt passwords, role-aware claims |
| **users** | 5 | CRUD, profiles, preferences |
| **itineraries** | 6 | Full CRUD, checkpoints, member forms, change events, publish workflow |
| **pricing** | 8 | Deterministic coupon engine, stacking rules, eligibility explanations, snapshots |
| **bookings** | 5 | Idempotent checkout, escrow hold, tender recording, journal entries |
| **notifications** | 5 | DND scheduling, templated messages, send logs (immutable), callback queue |
| **files** | 5 | AES-256-GCM encryption, time-limited download tokens, multipart upload |
| **finance** | 6 | Wallets, double-entry ledger, refunds ($1 min), courier withdrawals ($2,500 cap), reconciliation |
| **contracts** | 5 | Template rendering, contract/invoice generation |
| **procurement** | 5 | RFQ→Quote→PO→Delivery→Inspection→Discrepancy→Exception workflow |
| **reviews** | 5 | Multi-dimension ratings, credit tiers, violations, harassment flags |
| **risk** | 6 | Heuristic engine (>8 cancellations/24h, 20 RFQs/10m, harassment flags), throttling, blacklists |
| **admin** | 5 | Audit logs, send logs viewer, config, user management |

### Cross-Cutting
- Request ID middleware, structured zap logging, sensitive field masking
- JWT auth + RBAC middleware with `RequireRole()`
- Panic recovery, audit logging to DB
- Background worker (deferred notifications, token cleanup, risk recomputation)

### Frontend (React + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind)
- Login, Dashboard, role-aware navigation
- **Itinerary wizard** (7-step: title → date → location → checkpoints → notes → member form config → review)
- **Itinerary detail** with tabs (overview, checkpoints, members, forms, change history)
- **Booking pages** (list, create with line items, detail with coupon selector + pricing preview + checkout)
- **Notification center** with DND preferences
- **Procurement dashboard** with RFQ/PO/Exceptions tabs
- **Wallet dashboard** with transactions and withdrawal requests
- **Document center** with file upload and contract/invoice management
- **Review dashboard** with ratings and credit tier badges
- **Admin pages** (users, settings, overview)

### Database
- 60+ tables across 11 migrations covering all domain models
- UUID PKs, NUMERIC(18,2) for money, TIMESTAMPTZ, proper indexes
- Immutable append-only tables for audit_logs, send_logs, journal_entries
- Role/permission matrix fully seeded

### Seed Data
- 13 users across all 6 roles
- Sample itinerary with checkpoints and member forms
- 3 coupons (threshold, percentage, new-user gift)
- Sample booking, RFQ, quotes, PO with failed inspection
- Contract template, message templates, review dimensions, credit tiers

### To Run
```bash
# Start PostgreSQL
bash scripts/dev.sh db:up

# Copy env file
cp apps/api/.env.example apps/api/.env

# Start API (runs migrations automatically)
bash scripts/dev.sh api:run

# Seed data
bash scripts/dev.sh seed

# Start frontend dev server
bash scripts/dev.sh web:run
```

<result>All clean. Here is a summary of what was created:

**17 files created in `/c/Users/yonim/w2t80/repo/apps/api/`:**

1. **`go.mod`** - Module definition with all required dependencies (echo, pgx, jwt, zap, uuid, godotenv, bcrypt). `go mod tidy` pulled in all transitive dependencies and generated `go.sum`.

2. **`cmd/server/main.go`** - Entry point with env loading via godotenv, zap logger initialization (configurable level), pgx pool connection, migration execution, Echo setup via the App struct, and graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM.

3. **`internal/config/config.go`** - `Config` struct loaded from env vars with validation for required fields (DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, MASTER_ENCRYPTION_KEY) and sensible defaults for Port (8080), LogLevel (info), DownloadTokenTTL (15m), FileVaultPath (./vault).

4. **`internal/db/db.go`** - pgxpool connection setup with production-ready pool settings (25 max conns, 5 min conns, health checks, idle timeouts).

5. **`internal/db/migrate.go`** - Migration runner that tracks applied migrations in a `schema_migrations` table, reads `.sql` files from `infra/sql/migrations/` in sorted order, and executes each in a transaction.

6. **`internal/middleware/request_id.go`** - Generates UUID request IDs (or preserves incoming X-Request-ID), sets header and context value.

7. **`internal/middleware/logger.go`** - Structured zap logging with method, path, status, latency, request_id, remote_ip. Masks sensitive query params (token, password, secret, api_key). Log level varies by status code (error for 5xx, warn for 4xx).

8. **`internal/middleware/auth.go`** - JWT auth middleware that extracts Bearer tokens, validates via AuthService, sets user_id/email/roles in context. Supports exact and prefix-wildcard public path skipping.

9. **`internal/middleware/rbac.go`** - `RequireRole(roles ...string)` factory returning middleware that checks user context roles against required roles.

10. **`internal/middleware/recovery.go`** - Panic recovery that captures stack traces, logs with zap, and returns a 500 JSON error response.

11. **`internal/auth/service.go`** - AuthService with Login (queries DB, checks bcrypt hash, generates JWT), ValidateToken (parses HMAC-signed JWT), HashPassword/CheckPassword (bcrypt), GetUserByID. JWT claims include user_id, email, roles with 24h TTL.

12. **`internal/auth/handler.go`** - Handlers for POST /api/v1/auth/login, POST /api/v1/auth/logout, GET /api/v1/auth/me with input validation and standard response format.

13. **`internal/common/context.go`** - Helper functions: GetUserID, GetEmail, GetRoles, GetRequestID extracting typed values from Echo context.

14. **`internal/common/response.go`** - Standard API response envelope with Success, Created, Error, and ValidationError helpers.

15. **`internal/common/errors.go`** - DomainError type with code/message/wrapped error, plus constructor functions for NotFound, Conflict, Unauthorized, Forbidden, BadRequest, and Internal errors.

16. **`internal/app/router.go`** - Sets up Echo with middleware stack (recovery, request ID, logger, JWT auth), health/ready endpoints, and auth route group.

17. **`internal/app/app.go`** - App struct holding DB pool, logger, config, and all services/handlers. `New()` wires everything together.

All code compiles cleanly and passes `go vet`.</result>

<result>All 11 migration files created successfully in `/c/Users/yonim/w2t80/repo/infra/sql/migrations/`. Here is a summary:

| File | Contents |
|---|---|
| **001_extensions.sql** | Enables `uuid-ossp` and `pgcrypto` extensions |
| **002_users_auth.sql** | `users`, `roles` (seeded with 6 roles), `permissions`, `role_permissions`, `user_roles`, `user_profiles`, `user_preferences`, `do_not_disturb_settings`, `subscription_preferences`, `sessions` |
| **003_itineraries.sql** | `itineraries`, `itinerary_checkpoints`, `itinerary_members`, `itinerary_member_form_definitions`, `itinerary_member_form_submissions`, `itinerary_change_events` (append-only), `itinerary_notifications` |
| **004_bookings_orders.sql** | `bookings`, `booking_items`, `checkout_pricing_snapshots` |
| **005_coupons.sql** | `coupons`, `coupon_packs`, `coupon_pack_items`, `coupon_redemptions`, `pricing_memberships`, `idempotency_keys` |
| **006_finance.sql** | `wallets`, `wallet_transactions` (append-only), `escrow_accounts`, `payment_records`, `refunds`, `refund_items`, `withdrawal_requests`, `withdrawal_disbursements`, `journal_entries` (immutable), `journal_lines` (immutable), `counterparties`, `reconciliation_runs`, `reconciliation_items` |
| **007_notifications.sql** | `message_templates`, `messages`, `notification_events`, `notification_recipients`, `callback_queue_entries`, `send_logs` (immutable), `message_read_receipts` |
| **008_files_contracts.sql** | `file_metadata`, `file_record_links`, `file_access_policies`, `download_tokens`, `contract_templates`, `generated_contracts`, `invoice_requests`, `invoices` |
| **009_procurement.sql** | `rfqs`, `rfq_items`, `rfq_suppliers`, `rfq_quotes`, `rfq_quote_items`, `purchase_orders`, `po_items`, `deliveries`, `delivery_items`, `quality_inspections`, `discrepancy_tickets`, `exception_cases`, `waiver_records` (append-only), `settlement_adjustments` (append-only) |
| **010_reviews_risk.sql** | `reviews`, `review_dimensions`, `review_scores`, `credit_tiers`, `user_credit_snapshots` (append-only), `violation_records`, `harassment_flags`, `no_show_records`, `blacklist_records`, `risk_events`, `risk_scores`, `throttle_actions`, `admin_approvals` |
| **011_audit.sql** | `audit_logs` (immutable), `entity_versions` (immutable), `domain_events` (immutable) |

Key design decisions applied throughout:
- All PKs use `UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`
- All tables have `created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()`; mutable tables also have `updated_at`
- Money columns use `NUMERIC(18,2)`
- PostgreSQL enums for all status fields (`user_status`, `itinerary_status`, `booking_status`, `discount_type`, `wallet_type`, `escrow_status`, `tender_type`, `withdrawal_status`, `rfq_status`, `po_status`, `inspection_status`, `discrepancy_type`, `exception_status`, `invoice_request_status`, `credit_tier`)
- Partial unique index on `users.email` (only where `deleted_at IS NULL`), and on `blacklist_records` (active only)
- Append-only tables (`send_logs`, `journal_entries`, `journal_lines`, `audit_logs`, `entity_versions`, `domain_events`, `itinerary_change_events`, `waiver_records`, `settlement_adjustments`, `user_credit_snapshots`) have no `updated_at` or `deleted_at`
- All enums wrapped in `DO $$ ... EXCEPTION WHEN duplicate_object` for idempotent re-runs
- `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` and `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS` throughout
- Indexes on all foreign keys, status columns, and date columns used in common queries</result>
